description: American restaurateur
description_wiki: Clifford E. Clinton (August 3, 1900 – November 20, 1969) was a California restaurateur who founded Meals for Millions, one of two parent organizations of Freedom from Hunger, in 1946.

description_wiki: Louis Burt Mayer (; born Lazar Meir; July 12, 1884 – October 29, 1957) was a Canadian-American film producer and co-founder of Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer studios (MGM) in 1924.

description: public park, Los Angeles, United States
description_wiki: Hancock Park is a city park in the Miracle Mile section of the Mid-Wilshire neighborhood in Los Angeles, California.

entity: Los Angeles County Arboretum and Botanic Garden
surface_forms: State and County Arboretum in Arcadia
description: arboretum, botanical garden, and historical site in Arcadia, California, United States
description_wiki: The Los Angeles County Arboretum and Botanic Garden, 127 acres (51.4 ha), is an arboretum, botanical garden, and historical site nestled into hills near the San Gabriel Mountains in Arcadia, California, United States.

description: city in Los Angeles County, California, United States
description_wiki: Arcadia is a city in Los Angeles County, California, United States, located about 13 miles (21 km) northeast of downtown Los Angeles in the San Gabriel Valley and at the base of the San Gabriel Mountains.

description: Botanical garden in La Cañada Flintridge, California
description_wiki: Descanso Gardens is a 150-acre (61 ha) botanical garden located in La Cañada Flintridge, Los Angeles County, California.

description: city in Los Angeles County, California, United States
description_wiki: La Cañada Flintridge, commonly known as just La Cañada (Spanish for 'The Ravine'), is a city in the foothills of the Verdugo Mountains in Los Angeles County, California, United States.

description: thoroughfare in Los Angeles, United States
description_wiki: Olvera Street, commonly known by its Spanish name Calle Olvera, is a historic pedestrian street in El Pueblo de Los Ángeles, the historic center of Los Angeles.

description: memorial in Los Angeles, California, U.S
description_wiki: The Fort Moore Pioneer Memorial is a large stone memorial wall built in 1957 on part of the original location of Fort Moore (named for Benjamin D. Moore) facing North Hill Street, in Los Angeles, California.

description: American newspaper publisher (1899-1973)
description_wiki: Norman Chandler (September 14, 1899 – October 20, 1973) was the publisher of the Los Angeles Times from 1945 to 1960.

entity: Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints
surface_forms: Church of the Latter Day Saints
aliases: Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints
description: Christian restorationist denomination centered on the Book of Mormon
description_wiki: The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, informally known as the LDS Church or Mormon Church, is a Christian denomination and the largest denomination in the Latter Day Saint movement.

description: city in Los Angeles County, California, United States
description_wiki: Malibu (  MAL-ih-boo; Chumash: Humaliwo; Spanish: Malibú) is a beach city in the Santa Monica Mountains region of Los Angeles County, California, United States,  about 30 miles (48 km) west of Los Angeles.

description: American politician
description_wiki: John Anson Ford (September 29, 1883 – November 3, 1983) was an American journalist, advertising executive and Democratic Party politician.

description: American artist (1907-1989)
description_wiki: Millard Owen Sheets (June 24, 1907 – March 31, 1989) was an American artist, teacher, and architectural designer.

entity: Department of Water and Power (Los Angeles)
description: public electric and water utility serving Los Angeles, California
description_wiki: The Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP) is the largest municipal utility in the United States with 8,100 megawatts of electric generating capacity (2021–2022) and delivering an average of 435 million gallons of water per day (487,000 acre-ft per year) to more than four million residents and local businesses in the City of Los Angeles and several adjacent cities and communities in southwestern Los Angeles County, California.

description_wiki: The Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors (LACBOS) is the governing body of Los Angeles County, California, United States.

description: California governor (1911-17) and senator (1917-45)
description_wiki: Hiram Warren Johnson (September 2, 1866 – August 6, 1945) was an American attorney and politician who served as the 23rd governor of California from 1911 to 1917 and represented California in the U.S. Senate for five terms from 1917 to 1945.

description: American mayor
description_wiki: Fletcher Bowron (August 13, 1887 – September 11, 1968) was an American lawyer, judge, and politician.

description: harbor in Los Angeles, California
description_wiki: The Port of Los Angeles is a seaport managed by the Los Angeles Harbor Department, a unit of the City of Los Angeles.

description_wiki: The Statue of Liberty (Liberty Enlightening the World; French: La Liberté éclairant le monde) is a colossal neoclassical sculpture of a robed and crowned woman on Liberty Island in New York Harbor, within New York City.

description: 1776 assertion of colonial America's independence from Great Britain
description_wiki: The Declaration of Independence, formally The unanimous Declaration of the thirteen united States of America in the original printing, is the founding document of the United States.

description: California native-plant resource
description_wiki: The Theodore Payne Foundation for Wild Flowers and Native Plants — or TPF, is a private, non-profit organization founded in 1960 to promote the understanding and preservation of California native plants.

description: upper house of the United States Congress
description_wiki: The United States Senate is a chamber of the bicameral United States Congress; it is the upper house, and the U.S. House of Representatives is the lower house.

surface_forms: Los Angeles County Board of Education
aliases: The town of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of the Little Portion
description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.

aliases: Democratic Party of the United States
description: American political party
description_wiki: The Democratic Party is the major liberal political party in the United States, sitting on the center to center-left of the political spectrum.
